exemplar.title:
How does Mitski demonstrate the dysfunctional nature of the
unattainable standards placed on Asian Women for dignity and self-worth in western society
through juxtaposition, Point of view and motifs?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Clearly stated research question and identifiable analytical lenses
Adequate communication of topic with sufficient explanation
Recognition of primary texts and close reading approach
weaknesses: Topic statement wording is convoluted and at times obscures focus
Research question is densely phrased and only partially focused in the analysis
Methodology is very narrow and lacks systematic selection criteria or secondary sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the investigation establishes a clear focus on Mitskiâs lyrics and signals analytical lenses, the wording is often awkward and the link between devices and theme remains asserted rather than sharply defined. The research question is present but overly complex, and the methodology relies almost entirely on primary lyric analysis with minimal rationale or secondary scholarship. This performance falls at the lower end of the adequate Band 3â4 descriptors, meriting 2 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates a coherent understanding of key themes in Mitskiâs work
Uses a range of literary terminology (e.g., juxtaposition, motif) correctly in places
Supports points with direct quotations from song lyrics
weaknesses: Knowledge remains surface-level and is almost entirely drawn from primary texts
Secondary or scholarly sources are virtually absent, weakening claims
Technical vocabulary is sometimes applied loosely or ambiguously
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good familiarity with Mitskiâs lyrics and applies critical terms, but the understanding is limited by the absence of credible secondary sources and uneven precision in terminology. This places the work at the lower end of Band 3â4 for Knowledge and Understanding, justifying a score of 2 out of 6.

strengths: Close readings identify significant lyrical imagery and diction
Some effective analytical moments (e.g., juxtaposition in âHappyâ)
Clear thematic structure guiding the argument
weaknesses: Research base is extremely limited with no scholarly engagement
Analysis is uneven and often descriptive rather than critically evaluative
Conclusions and evaluation of findings are superficial, lacking counter-arguments
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay includes embryonic analysis and some well-supported observations, but overall the research is narrowly focused on primary lyrics, and the argument lacks depth and critical evaluation. These factors situate the work in the lower Band 4â6 descriptors for Critical Thinking, earning 3 out of 12.

strengths: All required structural elements are present (title page, contents, headings, works cited)
Section headings generally help orient the reader
weaknesses: Formatting is inconsistent (citation style irregular, spacing and punctuation errors)
Table of contents lacks uniform formatting, and key headings (e.g., Conclusion) are unlabeled
Layout conventions are applied only partially and erratically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the investigation meets the basic structural requirements, inconsistent application of formatting rules and lapses in layout undermine readability. The work falls into the lower Band 1â2 for Presentation, warranting 1 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The methodology is outlined mostly implicitly: the student selects seven songs and undertakes close textual (lyrical) analysis, supplemented by one brief biographical web source.  Some rationale for choosing particular tracks (ârawest project,â adolescent versus adult perspective) is offered, showing partial intentionality.

However, the range of sources is extremely narrowâlargely limited to primary lyrics with virtually no secondary scholarship on Mitski, music criticism, or theoretical frameworks on gender/race representation.  There is little evidence of systematic criteria for song selection or for the analytical tools employed, and no discussion of alternative approaches or research methods.

The limited scope and scant evidence of informed selection place the work in Band 1-2.  Given that at least a minimal methodology exists, a score of 2 (upper end of the band) is justified.

Basic layout conventions are observed: a title page, word count, page references in the table of contents, and a concluding works-cited list.  However, formatting is inconsistentâcitation style is irregular, album and song titles are not always correctly punctuated, and some headings, spacing and punctuation errors interrupt readability.  Evidence of sustained, correct application of an acknowledged academic style (MLA/APA/Chicago) is lacking.

These shortcomings indicate that while some layout requirements are addressed, accurate and consistent formatting has not been fully achieved.  The work therefore sits in Band 1-2 for this strand, meriting a score of 2 for partial but imperfect compliance.

The investigation draws almost all of its evidence from seven primary texts (song lyrics) and a single brief biographical webpage.  While these sources are relevant to the research question, the overall research base is very limited: no scholarly articles on Mitski, feminist literary criticism, or theoretical frameworks on race and gender are consulted.

Because the sources used are few and largely unquestioned, there is scant triangulation or critical engagement with existing scholarship.  Consequently, the research only partially illuminates the question and frequently relies on assertion rather than demonstrated evidence.

This aligns with Band 1-3, which describes "limited research with minimal relevance."  A score of 2 captures the slight but insufficient attempt to gather pertinent material.

The discussion follows a three-section structure (beauty standards, social norms, dysfunctional relationships) that echoes the research focus, giving the essay a discernible line of argument.  Nevertheless, the reasoning frequently becomes repetitive and descriptive, restating lyrical content rather than critically weighing different perspectives or acknowledging counter-arguments.

Evaluation of findings is minimal; the student seldom reflects on the implications or limitations of their readings, nor are other scholarly viewpoints incorporated to test interpretations.  As a result, the argument remains largely one-dimensional and only intermittently analytical.

These characteristics match Band 1-3, indicating limited argument development with superficial evaluation.  A score of 2 recognises some organisational logic but highlights the absence of sustained critical discussion.

The student does attempt close reading of lyrics, noting imagery, diction and symbolic contrasts to argue how Mitski critiques beauty standards, gender roles and relationship dynamics.  Several quotations are examined and connected to the central thesis, showing an embryonic analytical thread.

However, the depth is inconsistent: many claims remain at the level of thematic paraphrase (âthis shows western cultureâs objectification of womenâ) without exploring alternative interpretations, musical context, or broader theoretical insight.  Links between individual song analyses and the overarching argument are asserted rather than systematically developed, so conclusions are only partly supported.

These features fit the lower end of Band 4-6: analysis is present and sometimes effective but uneven and only mostlyârather than fullyâsupported.  A score of 4 reflects this adequate yet limited depth.

The essay contains all required structural elements: title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, distinct thematic chapters, conclusion and a works-cited list.  Headings and subheadings guide the reader, and the sequence broadly supports the unfolding argument, indicating an appropriate overall structure.

There are, however, some organisational weaknessesâsections occasionally overlap, and transitions are abrupt, causing minor repetition and loss of focus.  Nonetheless, the essential expectations for an EE in English A are met.

Given that the structure is "clearly appropriate" though not exemplary, the work fulfils Band 3-4; a score of 3 reflects sound but not seamless organisation.

The essay displays a generally clear familiarity with Mitskiâs biography and lyrics, and it provides song-specific quotations to support points.  The student shows an understanding of themes such as beauty standards, racialised othering and gender roles, applying them coherently across multiple tracks.

Nevertheless, the knowledge remains largely surface-level and is drawn almost exclusively from the songs themselves; contextual or critical sources that could enrich or corroborate claims are virtually absent.  This limits the sophistication and breadth of topic knowledge and weakens the authority of the discussion.

The performance therefore fits Band 3-4: good but not excellent knowledge, with only partially effective use of sources.  A score of 3 recognises solid song familiarity while acknowledging the thin secondary research base.

Throughout the essay the student employs literary-critical vocabularyââjuxtaposition,â âmotif,â âdiction,â âtone,â âvisual metaphor,â âallegory,â ârefrainââand applies these terms broadly correctly.  This demonstrates a working grasp of core English A: Language and Literature concepts.

Yet the precision and consistency of usage are uneven.  Some technical terms (e.g., âjuxtaposition,â âvisual metaphorâ) are applied rather loosely, and explanations occasionally blur literary and sociological terminology.  Stylistic infelicities and grammatical lapses sometimes obscure meaning, signaling partial, not authoritative, command of discourse conventions.

Given mostly accurate but not sustained or sophisticated deployment of terminology, the work merits placement in Band 3-4.  A score of 3 reflects the mid-range quality within that band.

The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and repeated in the introduction, demonstrating awareness of the need for a focused guiding inquiry.  It identifies a clear corpus (selected songs from Lush and Puberty 2) and specific literary devices to be examined.

Nonetheless, the question is densely worded, blending several abstract concepts (âdysfunctional nature,â âunattainable standards,â âdignity and self-worthâ) so that the precise analytical angle risks becoming diffuse.  In practice, the discussion often drifts into broad social commentary rather than maintaining tight alignment with the question.

Because the question is present, recognisable and generally linked to the essay, but only partially focused and consistently applied, the work matches Band 3-4.  A score of 3 reflects its moderate but uneven focus and connection to the ensuing analysis.

annotations.comment: The citation for the NPR website is incomplete and inconsistent with academic standards. Include author/date details and standardize URL formatting to ensure credibility and traceability.

The topic statement employs convoluted language and redundancy, which obscures the central focus. Simplify phrasing and explicitly connect each analytical lens (juxtaposition, point of view, motifs) to the core theme for clearer communication of purpose.
Terminology such as âvisual metaphorâ and âtoneâ is introduced but applied loosely. Define and apply literary terms precisely to demonstrate a robust grasp of critical vocabulary.
The bibliography lists only primary materials and one website. Add secondary academic sourcesâsuch as journal articles or critical essaysâto demonstrate comprehensive research.
Citation style for lyrics is inconsistent and lacks standard formatting (e.g., album title, track number). Adopt a consistent MLA/APA/Chicago style for all musical references.
Though the text functions as a conclusion, it lacks the explicit âConclusionâ heading required in the structure. Label this section clearly to meet formal expectations.
Works Cited entries display inconsistent spelling of artist name and formatting of titles and publishers. Standardize each citation entry to adhere to one style guide.

The research question is densely packed with abstract terms, making it feel diffuse. Consider narrowing and streamlining the wording to sharpen its focus and ensure consistent alignment with the ensuing analysis.
The section heading for âBeauty standardsâ is clearly distinguished, aiding reader orientation and signalling the start of a focused thematic analysis.
The table of contents lacks consistent dot leaders and indentation, which hinders readability. Apply uniform formatting to improve visual hierarchy and ease of navigation.
Analysis describes thematic content well but does not explicitly connect examples to the technique of juxtaposition. Anchor interpretations in the analytical lens to deepen the critical argument.
The closing discussion summarizes themes but offers limited critical evaluation or reflection on broader implications. Deepen the conclusion by synthesizing findings and addressing limitations.
The introduction heading is clear, but the section would benefit from a brief structural roadmap outlining the major sections and analytical steps to guide the reader.
The opening paragraph is overly long and contains run-on sentences, which reduces clarity. Break into concise statements and focus each sentence on a single idea to strengthen coherence and flow.

The methodology for song selection is described only implicitly and lacks systematic criteria or theoretical framework. Explicitly justify choices and reference secondary scholarship to demonstrate informed selection.
The phrase âlike microphone permission you thoseâ is unclear and appears to be a writing or OCR error. Revise or clarify this sentence to convey the intended meaning.
Song citations again lack consistency and necessary details. Standardize bibliography entries for each lyric quote and include track/album metadata.
The close reading of âHappyâ effectively identifies the juxtaposition between innocence and exploitation, demonstrating emerging analytical skill in linking lyrics to social critique.
The âDysfunctional Relationshipsâ header formatting differs from earlier themes. Align style (bold, italics, size) with previous headings for structural consistency.

Analysis of âOnce More To See Youâ describes the scenario but fails to tie insights back to the central question about unattainable standards. Reinforce how each example supports your overarching thesis.

Discussion of âYour Best American Girlâ is rich in description but stops short of critically evaluating implications or considering alternative interpretations. Introduce evaluative commentary to deepen the argument.
The styling of this section header deviates from earlier headings (missing bold/italic). Ensure uniform heading formatting throughout to maintain professional presentation.
Lyrics continue to be quoted without clear attribution format. Consistently apply an academic citation style with complete information for every track.
The analysis relies solely on primary lyrics and one website. Incorporate secondary scholarly sources on Mitski, gender theory or music criticism to strengthen the knowledge base.
